title: viola odorata subsp. stolonifera
intro: Viola odorata subsp. stolonifera, also known as the sweet violet or common violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant that is native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and delicate purple flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Viola odorata subsp. stolonifera include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, from minor cuts and scrapes to more serious conditions like eczema and acne.
